old_claim.note: Maritime Pilot Books provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Port Cranes & Harbor Works and Maritime Pilot Books; Port cranes and harbor works are physical trade infrastructure and do not require written pilot books as a direct prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Guild source supports medieval trade organization context; harbor works do not require written sailing directions as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Port cranes and harbor works are physical trade infrastructure and do not require written pilot books as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Port Cranes & Harbor Works claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Maritime Pilot Books as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Port Cranes & Harbor Works treated Maritime Pilot Books as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Port Cranes & Harbor Works no longer directly depends on Maritime Pilot Books; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: port_crane_harbor_works must not directly depend on maritime_pilot_books unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows port_crane_harbor_works is intentionally scoped only to cases that require maritime_pilot_books.
The graph splits port_crane_harbor_works and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on maritime_pilot_books.
A stronger source demonstrates that maritime_pilot_books is chronologically and causally necessary for port_crane_harbor_works.
short_reason: Port cranes and harbor works are physical trade infrastructure and do not require written pilot books as a direct prerequisite.
